questions: The uncommitted changes appear to intentionally revert the top drop zone feature that was just added in commits f93683867 and 1d33ef8a1. Was there a specific issue with that implementation (visual glitch, wrong insertion behavior, detection threshold too sensitive) that prompted the revert? If so, the plan should address that root cause rather than just restoring the code.

For the top drop indicator visual: the committed version shows a dashed sky-blue bar with text 'Drop to create new visual track at top'. Should this match the bottom drop zone styling exactly, or is a different treatment desired (e.g., thinner line, different color, just an arrow)?

Should the top drop zone activation threshold be symmetric with the bottom? Currently the bottom zone activates when the cursor is below the last track row. The top zone activates when rawRowIndex < 0 (cursor above the first track's top edge). Should there be a larger 'hot zone' at the top to make it easier to trigger?
success_criteria: Effect-layer clips (2 tabs) show tabs stretched evenly across full panel width, not bunched to the left.
All other clip types (3-tab audio, 4-tab visual/text) continue to render tabs correctly with even spacing.
Dragging any droppable item (file, text tool, effect layer, asset) above the first track shows a top drop indicator.
Dropping on the top indicator creates a new track at index 0 and shifts existing tracks down.
The bottom drop zone continues to work identically to before (no regression).
The top indicator label correctly reflects the track kind (visual vs audio) based on what's being dragged.
assumptions: The committed top drop zone implementation (HEAD) is functionally correct and the uncommitted revert was exploratory or accidental — so restoring it is the right starting point.
Track ordering is purely index-based (tracks[0] = topmost visual track). Prepending to the arrays is sufficient to create a track 'at the top'.
The tab fix only needs TAB_COLUMNS_CLASS updates — no changes to the shared Tabs component or TabsList base styles are needed.
Internal clip dragging (DnD Kit-based) is separate from external drop handling and won't be affected by these changes.
The string-based label matching in TimelineCanvas ('includes at top') is acceptable tech debt for now since the label format is fully controlled by DropIndicator.
structure_warnings: Each step section should reference at least one file in backticks.
